title: Upload Admission, Untrusted Files, and Quarantine · Go Semantics Case: Filename Normalization
summary: An upload handler trusts client filenames and content types, accepts unlimited bodies, publishes bytes before scanning, and leaves partial state when the caller disconnects. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the filename normalization increment in bounded upload-admission and quarantine workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Treat filenames as display metadata, normalize safely, reject controls and path confusion, and generate storage keys independently.
Enforce per-file, request, tenant, concurrent, time, and aggregate byte budgets before and during transfer.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Filename Normalization · Theory Filename Normalization
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 5267 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound upload bytes and concurrency before quarantined publication state; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat filenames as display metadata, normalize safely, reject controls and path confusion, and generate storage keys independently.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Filename Normalization contract for the supplied data.
